title: aylthonia exscapa
intro: Aylthonia exscapa, also known as Sea Sandwort, is a small, annual or biennial plant that belongs to the caryophyllaceae family. It is native to the coastal regions of Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is often found growing in sandy or rocky areas. The medicinal properties of Aylthonia exscapa include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing coughs, and promoting digestive health.
